evals.name: Integer DDC MATLAB Function
evals.description: Design a complete integer-decimation DDC as a MATLAB function suitable for HDL code generation
evals.prompt: Write a MATLAB function for HDL code generation that implements a DDC (Digital Down Converter). Specs: Fs=100 MHz input, Fc=25 MHz carrier to remove, total decimation of 16x (CIC x8 then FIR x2). Use 16-bit signed input with 14 fractional bits. Include a testbench script that feeds 16000 samples of a 1 MHz test signal through the DDC and prints the number of output samples. Use dsp.CICCompensationDecimator to design the FIR coefficients (4 CIC sections, diff delay 1, 2 MHz signal bandwidth).

context.setup: Requires DSP System Toolbox, DSP HDL Toolbox, Fixed-Point Designer

assertions.description: Uses dsphdl.NCO System object
Uses dsphdl.CICDecimator System object
Uses dsphdl.FIRDecimator System object
NCO phase increment is negative (required for down-conversion)
assertions.expected: phaseInc computed with negation: round((-Fc * 2^AccWL) / Fs) or equivalent negative formula

assertions.description: Mixer does NOT use conj() on NCO output
Valid signal piped through chain (no if-statements on valid)
assertions.expected: ncoValid feeds cicDec, cicValid feeds firDec — no conditional gating

assertions.description: All System objects declared persistent in HDL function
assertions.expected: persistent keyword used for all dsphdl objects

assertions.description: FIR NumCycles set to CIC decimation factor (8)
assertions.expected: NumCycles = 8 on FIR decimator (or cicDecimFactor variable)

assertions.description: Test signal is sinusoid modulated onto complex carrier
assertions.expected: cos(2*pi*Fsig*t) .* exp(1j*2*pi*Fc*t) pattern for input signal

assertions.description: The complete DDC function and testbench run without error

evals.name: Non-Integer DDC with Farrow
evals.description: Design a DDC with non-integer total decimation using Farrow rate converter for fractional adjustment
evals.prompt: Design a DDC with non-integer decimation. Specs: Fs=122.88 MHz, Fc=30 MHz, desired output rate=15 MHz (total decimation = 8.192). Use CIC(x4) + FIR(x2) for integer stages and a Farrow rate converter for the fractional remainder. 16-bit signed input, 14 fractional. Write the HDL function and a testbench that verifies it produces output. Use dsp.CICCompensationDecimator for FIR design (4 CIC sections, 2 MHz bandwidth).

context.setup: Requires DSP System Toolbox, DSP HDL Toolbox, Fixed-Point Designer

assertions.description: Uses dsphdl.FarrowRateConverter
Farrow RateChange is greater than 1 (decimation in DDC, since RateChange = fsIn/fsOut)
Integer stages (CIC*FIR) do not exceed total decimation
Farrow outputs captured as 3 values [data, valid, ready]
assertions.expected: Three output capture from farrow() call, ready discarded with ~

assertions.description: Farrow OutputDataType set to 'Same as first input'
DDC function and testbench run without error

evals.name: DDC Parameter Computation
evals.description: Verify correct computation of DDC parameters including negative phase increment and staging
evals.prompt: Write a MATLAB script that computes DDC parameters for: Fs=250 MHz, Fc=70 MHz, total decimation=64 (CIC x16, FIR1 x2, FIR2 x2). Use 32-bit NCO accumulator. Compute and print: (1) the phase increment value, (2) actual resolved frequency, (3) CIC bit growth for 16-bit input with 5 sections and diff delay 1, (4) NumCycles for each FIR stage, (5) output sample rate.

assertions.description: Phase increment = round((-70e6 * 2^32) / 250e6) = -1202590843
CIC bit growth = N*ceil(log2(R*M)) = 5*ceil(log2(16*1)) = 5*4 = 20 bits
Output sample rate = 250e6/64 = 3.90625 MHz
FIR1 NumCycles=16 (CIC dec factor), FIR2 NumCycles=32 (16*2)
Script runs without error

evals.name: High Decimation Three-Stage (CIC + FIR + FIR)
evals.description: Verify that a high decimation factor (128x) produces a three-stage CIC + 2 FIR architecture with correct NumCycles on each FIR
evals.prompt: Design a DDC for HDL code generation. Specs: Fs=256 MHz, Fc=64 MHz, total decimation=128 (use CIC x32, FIR1 x2, FIR2 x2). Input is 16-bit signed, 14 fractional bits. Use 5 CIC sections, diff delay 1. Design the compensation FIR with dsp.CICCompensationDecimator (1 MHz signal bandwidth). Use designMultirateFIR for the second FIR stage. Write the HDL function and a testbench.

context.setup: Requires DSP System Toolbox, DSP HDL Toolbox, Fixed-Point Designer

assertions.description: Uses three-stage architecture: CIC + FIR + FIR
assertions.expected: dsphdl.CICDecimator, dsphdl.FIRDecimator (first), dsphdl.FIRDecimator (second) — three distinct decimation stages

assertions.description: CIC decimation factor is 32
First FIR NumCycles = 32 (CIC decimation factor)
Second FIR NumCycles = 64 (CIC * FIR1 = 32 * 2)
First FIR uses dsp.CICCompensationDecimator coefficients (not fir1 or designMultirateFIR)
assertions.expected: dsp.CICCompensationDecimator used to design first FIR coefficients

assertions.description: Second FIR uses designMultirateFIR for channel-select filter
assertions.expected: designMultirateFIR used for second FIR coefficients

assertions.description: All stages use OutputDataType 'Same word length as input'
assertions.expected: OutputDataType = 'Same word length as input' on CIC and both FIR stages

assertions.description: Valid signal piped through all three decimation stages
assertions.expected: ncoValid → CIC, cicValid → FIR1, fir1Valid → FIR2 — no conditional gating

assertions.description: HDL function and testbench run without error

evals.name: Integer Staging — No Farrow Offered
evals.description: Verify that integer total decimation factors are correctly identified and only integer staging is offered
evals.prompt: I want to design a DDC with Fs=100 MHz, Fc=10 MHz, and output rate of 4 MHz. What are the decimation staging options? Just list the options with the factors for each stage — don't write full code yet.

assertions.description: Identifies total decimation of 25 as integer (100/4=25)
Does NOT offer Farrow-based options for integer decimation
All offered stage factors multiply to exactly 25
assertions.expected: Every option's factors multiply to 25 (e.g., 5*5, 25*1 not valid since 1 is not decimation)

assertions.description: No factor-1 (non-decimating) stages offered

evals.name: CIC Compensation Filter Design
evals.description: Verify that CIC compensation filter uses dsp.CICCompensationDecimator, not fir1 or other generic lowpass
evals.prompt: Design a CIC compensation FIR filter for a DDC. The CIC has: decimation factor 12, 4 sections, differential delay 1. The FIR should decimate by 2 after the CIC. Sample rate after CIC is 20 MHz. Passband is 500 kHz, stopband at 833 kHz. Give me the MATLAB code to design the filter and extract its coefficients.

assertions.description: Uses dsp.CICCompensationDecimator to design the filter
assertions.expected: dsp.CICCompensationDecimator instantiated with the CIC parameters

assertions.description: Does NOT use fir1() for compensation filter design
CICRateChangeFactor matches the CIC decimation factor (12)
CICNumSections matches the CIC sections (4)
Decimation factor on the compensation filter is 2
Coefficients extracted via .coeffs.Numerator or coeffs() function
assertions.expected: Coefficients extracted from the designed filter object
